title: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate brings near real-time voice translation to Google products
friendlyTitle: Google’s new Gemini voice translation works closer to real time
summary: Google announced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech model that automatically detects more than 70 languages and produces natural translated audio while staying only a few seconds behind the speaker. It is rolling out through Google AI Studio, the Gemini Live API, Google Translate, and private preview in Google Meet.
friendlySummary: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate can listen to speech in more than 70 languages and speak the translation a few seconds later. It is coming to developer tools, Google Translate, and some Google Meet customers.
whyItMatters: Live translation is one of the clearest daily-life AI use cases: meetings, travel, education, support calls, and multilingual teams all benefit when language barriers shrink without forcing rigid turn-taking.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could make travel, meetings, classes, and customer calls easier when people do not share a language. It is a practical example of AI helping daily communication.
